nodes.description: Argentine national football team, won the 2022 FIFA World Cup and secured their third title | Argentina is a nation that advanced to the semi-finals of the World Cup and won against Croatia with a score of 3-0 | Argentina won against Mexico but lost to Poland, advancing to the knockout stage with a goal difference of 1. | Argentina is a nation that won the 2022 FIFA World Cup, their third title overall | Argentina is a national football team that won the World Cup in 2022 | Argentina topped Group A with 6 points and advanced to the knockout stage | Argentina is a nation that participated in the tournament with its national team. Lionel Messi was named the best player of the tournament. | Argentina advanced to the semi-finals by beating Croatia in extra time and penalties, but lost to the Netherlands in the final.

France is a nation participating in the 2022 FIFA World Cup and is represented by Kylian Mbappé, the captain of their national team | France is a nation that reached the final of the 2022 FIFA World Cup and lost on penalties to Argentina in the final | France is a nation that qualified for the knockout stage of the World Cup and won against Australia with four goals. | France is a defending champion who reached their second consecutive World Cup semi-final and defeated Morocco 2-0 in the semi-finals | France is a national football team that lost their opening game but went on to win the 2022 World Cup | French national football team, achieved a second World Cup victory after 20 years | France is a nation that has won the tournament since 2002. It also hosts the French national team and is part of UEFA.

Qatar hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup, their first time hosting the event | Host nation of the 2022 FIFA World Cup and a Middle Eastern country known for its oil wealth and modern infrastructure | Qatar is a country that hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup and has faced criticism regarding labor practices. | Qatar is a country that hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup, being the first Arab nation to do so. | Qatar is a country that hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup. It faced criticism over its treatment of migrant workers and women, as well as concerns about climate conditions and scheduling changes. | Qatar is hosting the 2022 FIFA World Cup and has no LGBTQ rights, with homosexuality criminalised | Qatar is a nation hosting the 2022 FIFA World Cup, known for its restrictions on LGBTQ+ rights and limited freedoms for women | Qatar is the host nation of the 2022 FIFA World Cup and entered the event as their national team | Qatar is a country that successfully bid to host the 2022 World Cup but has faced scrutiny over human rights issues and allegations of corruption. | Al Bayt Stadium, Al Khor hosted matches of Group A and was one of the venues for the 2022 FIFA World Cup | Qatar is a country that hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup and faces criticism regarding women's rights. | Qatar hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup and was eliminated in the group stage. It is a nation that competed in the tournament. | Qatar hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup and implemented measures for alcohol consumption and fan safety | Qatar is a country that hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup and was involved in bidding processes for the tournament | Qatar is a country that successfully bid for and hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup, facing criticism over various issues including security incidents and allegations of corruption. | Qatar is a country that successfully bid for and will host the 2022 FIFA World Cup | Qatar organized the 2026 World Cup and faced criticism for banning kosher food and public prayer services for Jewish tourists.> | Qatar is a country that bid to host the 2022 FIFA World Cup and won, with significant investments in infrastructure including stadiums. | Qatar hosted the FIFA World Cup and aimed to comply with the Global Sustainability Assessment System (GSAS) for all World Cup stadiums. Seven out of eight stadiums were built or renovated, with Al Khor being an exception.

Croatian national football team, defeated Morocco 2-1 in a match that finished 0-0 in their opening game | Croatia was defeated by Argentina 1-3 in the quarter-finals and did not advance further | Croatia was eliminated from the tournament after losing to Argentina in the quarter-finals via a penalty shootout. | Croatia is a team that played in the group stage and was eliminated by Morocco | Croatia is a team that lost to France in the 2018 FIFA World Cup Final, defending champions of the tournament. | Croatia finished third in the 2022 FIFA World Cup

Morocco became the first African nation to reach the semi-final of a World Cup, losing to France in the quarter-finals | Morocco became the first African and Arab nation to advance to the semi-finals of the World Cup, but lost to France 2-0 | Moroccan national football team, secured fourth place and the best World Cup finish for an African or Arab nation | Morocco is a team that qualified for the tournament and played against Croatia, Belgium, and Canada

A country within the United Kingdom that played against Iran in the 2022 FIFA World Cup | England lost to France 1-2 in the quarter-finals, with Harry Kane scoring a penalty | England received the Fair Play award at the 2022 FIFA World Cup | England is a team that advanced to the knockout stage after winning their group in the 2022 FIFA World Cup | England is a team that qualified for the knockout stage with 7 points
Kylian Mbappé is a French professional footballer who won the Golden Boot for scoring the most goals in the 2022 FIFA World Cup
Argentine professional footballer who scored two goals in the final against France | Lionel Messi is an Argentine player and was named the Best Player of the 2022 FIFA World Cup

Enzo Fernández, another teammate of Kylian Mbappé on the French national team, won the Young Player Award for being the best young player in the tournament. | Enzo Fernández, a young player from Argentina, won the Best Young Player award at the 2022 FIFA World Cup
Emiliano Martínez is an Argentine goalkeeper who received the Best Goalkeeper award at the 2022 FIFA World Cup

A specific edition of the World Cup held in Qatar in 2022 with unique rules and prize structures | The 2022 FIFA World Cup is an international football tournament held in Qatar | The 22nd FIFA World Cup held in Qatar, where various controversies and incidents occurred | The 2022 FIFA World Cup was a quadrennial world championship for national football teams organised by FIFA, held in Qatar from November 20 to December 18, 2022

French professional footballer and captain of the French national team, known for his speed and goal-scoring ability | Kylian Mbappé is a French professional footballer who became the first player to score a hat-trick in a World Cup final since 1966. He also won the Golden Boot for scoring the most goals (eight) during the tournament.

FIFA is the organizer of the FIFA World Cup and approved a smaller number of stadiums due to growing costs in Qatar. By 2017, FIFA had yet to finalize the number of stadiums required for the tournament. | Federation Internationale de Football Association organizes and manages international football competitions including the World Cup | Fedration Internationale de Football Association, organiser of the World Cup | Federation Internationale de Football Association, organiser of the FIFA World Cup and other sporting events | Federation Internationale de Football Association, organiser of the World Cup tournament | Federation Internationale de Football Association organises and manages the FIFA World Cup, which is held every four years between national football teams. | Federation Internationale de Football Association organises and manages international football competitions including the World Cup | Federation Internationale de Football Association, organiser of the World Cup and other international football competitions | Fédération Internationale de Football Association, organiser of the World Cup | The Federation Internationale de Football Association organizes the FIFA World Cup and was involved in selecting the hosts of both the 2018 and 2022 tournaments. | Federation Internationale de Football Association, organiser of various football competitions including the World Cup | The international governing body of football, responsible for organizing major tournaments like the World Cup.
Lionel Messi, an Argentine professional footballer, won the Golden Ball as the best player of the tournament and is considered one of the greatest players of all time.
Emiliano Martínez, a teammate of Kylian Mbappé on the French national team, won the Golden Glove as the best goalkeeper in the tournament.

Brazil is a team that advanced to the knockout stage of Group F | Brazil also reached the semi-finals but lost to France, and their match against Croatia went into extra time and penalties. | Brazil is a team that advanced to the knockout stage and won against Serbia with Richarlison scoring two goals | Brazil is a nation and football powerhouse with a rich history in the World Cup, though not mentioned as participating in this specific event.

Germany is a team that won against Spain and Costa Rica, securing second place in their group but failing to advance to the knockout stages | Germany is another strong team that participated in the tournament but did not make it to the final stages.
Geoff Hurst is an English footballer who scored a hat-trick in the 1966 World Cup Final.
The FIFA World Cup 2022 was held in Qatar from November to December 2022.

Al Bayt Stadium is located in Al Khor and was used for one of the matches between Belgium and Canada | Al Bayt Stadium was used for the match between France and Morocco with an attendance of 68,294 | Located in Al Khor, this stadium hosted the England vs. United States match | Al Bayt Stadium is one of the venues used for the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar. | Al Khor, Qatar based stadium that hosted a match for third place | Al Bayt Stadium is an indoor stadium built for the World Cup with a seating capacity of 68,000. Its upper tiers will be disassembled after the tournament and donated to countries with less developed sports infrastructure. | Al Bayt Stadium is one of the stadiums that will be moderately or heavily modified after the tournament to reduce its capacity by half, used for the 2022 World Cup.

Located in Al Rayyan, Qatar, this stadium hosted matches between Japan and Costa Rica, and Germany and Japan | Stadium in Al Rayyan, Qatar where the final was played | Located in Al Rayyan, this stadium hosted matches between England and Iran | Khalifa International Stadium also served as a venue for the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar. | Khalifa International Stadium is located in Al Rayyan and hosted the match between Croatia and Canada | Khalifa International Stadium was heavily renovated for the tournament and is the largest stadium in the country at the time of its renovation. | Khalifa International Stadium in Al Rayyan hosted multiple matches of the 2022 FIFA World Cup, including those from Group B | Khalifa International Stadium is a largely unmodified stadium in Doha, used for the 2022 World Cup. It was not dismantled or heavily modified post-tournament.

Al Thumama Stadium was another stadium used during the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar. | Al Thumama Stadium was the venue for the match between England and France with an attendance of 68,895 | Situated in Doha, it was where the Iran vs. United States game took place | Al Thumama Stadium is a new stadium in Doha, commissioned in May 2017 and completed in 2024. It was used for the 2021 FIFA Arab Cup but may not be dismantled after the tournament. | Al Thumama Stadium is located in Doha and was used for the match between Belgium and Morocco | Al Thumama Stadium in Doha served as a venue for several matches during the 2022 FIFA World Cup, including Group A games

Ahmad bin Ali Stadium is located in Al Rayyan and hosted two matches: Belgium vs. Canada and Croatia vs. Canada | Situated in Al Rayyan, it was the venue for the match between United States and Wales | Ahmad bin Ali Stadium is one of the stadiums that will be moderately or heavily modified after the tournament to reduce its capacity by half, used for the 2022 World Cup. | Ahmad bin Ali Stadium hosted matches for the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar.

Lusail Stadium hosted matches in the quarter-finals including Argentina vs Croatia and France vs Morocco | Lusail Stadium was used as the venue for the final match of the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar, held on National Day. | Lusail Stadium is a new stadium built for the World Cup and completed in November 2021. | Lusail Stadium hosted matches for teams including Switzerland, Brazil, and Cameroon | Stadium in Lusail, Qatar where the match between France and Morocco was played | The venue for Argentina's match against Saudi Arabia on 22 November 2022 | Lusail Stadium is one of the stadiums that will be moderately or heavily modified after the tournament to reduce its capacity by half, used for the 2022 World Cup. | Lusail Stadium hosted the match between Portugal and Uruguay in the knockout stage of the tournament. | The main stadium for the final match of the 2022 FIFA World Cup, one of eight new stadiums built specifically for the event | Lusail Stadium hosted matches between Argentina and Saudi Arabia, Poland and Saudi Arabia, and France and Mexico.

Stadium 974 is one of the venues that hosted matches during the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar. | Stadium 974 hosted matches for teams including Uruguay and Ghana | Stadium 974 is a temporary stadium in Doha, completed on 30 November 2021. It hosted matches in the 2021 FIFA Arab Cup and was supposed to be dismantled after the tournament but remained abandoned as of November 2024. | Located in Doha, this stadium hosted the Mexico vs. Poland game

Education City Stadium was a venue for some matches during the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar. | Education City Stadium hosted a match between Morocco and Portugal with an attendance of 44,198 | Education City Stadium is a venue where several matches took place during the tournament, including Uruguay vs South Korea and Portugal vs Ghana. | Education City Stadium is one of the stadiums that will be moderately or heavily modified after the tournament to reduce its capacity by half, used for the 2022 World Cup.

Al Janoub Stadium was used for a match involving Ghana and Uruguay, which Uruguay won 2-0. | Al Janoub Stadium hosted matches for teams including Cameroon, Serbia, and Brazil | Al Janoub Stadium hosted matches for the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar. | Al Janoub Stadium is a new stadium in Al Wakrah, completed on 16 May 2019. It was used for the 2021 FIFA Arab Cup and will be modified after the tournament.
The match between Qatar and Ecuador was moved to November 20, 2022, due to Qatar's request to open the tournament with their team.

The 2026 FIFA World Cup took place in Qatar, where various incidents involving political and religious tensions occurred among fans and officials.> | The premier international football tournament held every four years, featuring national teams from around the world

Russia is a country whose Football Union (RFU) faced suspension from FIFA for international competitions | Russia was also a bidder for hosting the 2018 and 2022 FIFA World Cups but did not host either event due to corruption allegations | A former Soviet republic that bid for hosting rights to the 2018 FIFA World Cup but did not succeed

Iran played against United States in the 2022 FIFA World Cup but did not participate in Group A matches. | A country with anti-government protests, whose government faces criticism for suppressing such protests at the 2022 FIFA World Cup. | Iran is a team that participated in Group B of the 2022 FIFA World Cup and was eliminated from the tournament | Iran is a country that hosted protests against its Islamic government during the 2026 World Cup, with fans displaying slogans like "Woman, Life, Freedom".> | An Asian nation with a national football team that participated in the 2022 FIFA World Cup and faced England in their opening match | Iran finished third in Group B with 3 points and was eliminated from the tournament
Iranian goalkeeper who suffered a concussion during his team's opening match and was replaced by Hossein Hosseini
A substitute goalkeeper for Iran, replacing Alireza Beiranvand after the latter suffered a concussion in their World Cup match against England
Russian President who handed over the symbolic relay baton to Qatar's Emir Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani for hosting rights of the 2018 FIFA World Cup
Qatar's Emir who received the symbolic relay baton from Russian President Vladimir Putin for hosting rights of the 2022 FIFA World Cup

Union of European Football Associations, which guaranteed to host the 2018 FIFA World Cup and thus was not in contention for 2022 | UEFA, the Union of European Football Associations, guaranteed to host the 2018 FIFA World Cup and thus were no longer in contention for hosting the 2022 event.

Mexico drew against Poland and lost to Argentina, finishing third in Group D. | A country that initially bid for hosting rights to the 2018 FIFA World Cup but withdrew from proceedings | Mexico finished third in Group A with 4 points, securing a spot in the round of 16

Indonesia attempted to bid for hosting the 2022 FIFA World Cup but its bid was rejected by FIFA due to a lack of government support. | A Southeast Asian nation that submitted a bid for hosting the 2018 FIFA World Cup, which was rejected by FIFA due to lack of government support

The United States was one of the bidders for hosting the 2022 FIFA World Cup but did not win the bid. | United States participated in the 2022 FIFA World Cup and was eliminated in the group stage. It did not play in Group A matches. | The United States is a team that qualified for the round of 16 after defeating Iran and drawing with England in Group B | The United States secured their place in the round of 16 with 5 points

South Korea is a team that finished second in Group H and advanced to the knockout stage | South Korea also bid to host the 2022 FIFA World Cup but was eliminated in an early round of voting. | South Korea also finished in their group with 4 points but did not progress as they had the same number of points as Uruguay and fewer goals scored.

Japan participated in bidding for hosting the 2022 FIFA World Cup but withdrew its bid before the final vote. | Japan secured first place in Group E by defeating Spain and Germany, advancing to the knockout stage of the 2022 FIFA World Cup | Japan was a bidder for hosting the 2022 FIFA World Cup but withdrew its bid before the final vote.

Australia was a bidder for hosting the 2022 FIFA World Cup but was eliminated early on in the voting process. | Australia is a nation that defeated Tunisia and Denmark to advance as runners-up in Group D, securing their place in the knockout round. | Australia is a nation competing in the 2022 FIFA World Cup
Zürich, Switzerland is where the 22-member FIFA Executive Committee convened on December 2, 2010, to vote for the hosts of both the 2018 and 2022 World Cups.
The FIFA Executive Committee is responsible for making decisions regarding the hosting of major football events such as the World Cup, including voting on bids from various nations.
The 2018 FIFA World Cup, hosted by Russia, concluded before the bidding process for the 2022 event began.

Qatar's World Cup chief executive of the Supreme Committee for Delivery and Legacy, who discussed the creation of sobering-up zones during the 2022 FIFA World Cup | Nasser Al Khater is the CEO of Qatar's organizing committee and has commented on the cost of hosting the 2022 World Cup.
One of the proposed stadiums for the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar, designed to reflect Qatari history and culture with sustainability features.
Another stadium planned for the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar, focusing on legacy, comfort, accessibility, and sustainability.
A third proposed stadium for the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar, with similar design goals as other stadiums.
The fourth proposed stadium for the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar, aiming to meet sustainability and infrastructure development standards.
The fifth proposed stadium for the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar, designed with cooling systems and other sustainable features.
Al Rayyan is one of the five Qatari cities where stadiums were built or renovated for the FIFA World Cup, including Ahmad bin Ali Stadium and Education City Stadium.
Doha is a city in Qatar where several stadiums were built or renovated for the FIFA World Cup, including Al Thumama Stadium and Stadium 974.

Senegal is a team that qualified for the knockout round of Group A in the 2022 FIFA World Cup | Senegal participated in the 2022 FIFA World Cup and qualified as runners-up of Group A after defeating Ecuador in the final match of the group stage.

Ecuador participated in the 2022 FIFA World Cup and was eliminated in the group stage. It lost to Senegal in the final match of the group stage. | Ecuador is a team that played against Senegal and also qualified for the knockout round of Group A in the 2022 FIFA World Cup

The Netherlands is a team that advanced to the knockout stage after winning their group in the 2022 FIFA World Cup | The Netherlands reached the final of the tournament after defeating Argentina in the semi-finals. | The Netherlands won their group in the 2022 FIFA World Cup, defeating Qatar and Ecuador to secure a place in the knockout rounds.

A group comprising England, United States, Wales, Iran, Argentina, Poland, Mexico, and Saudi Arabia for the initial round matches | Group A was one of the groups in the 2022 FIFA World Cup where teams competed for qualification to the knockout stage

A group consisting of Argentina, Mexico, Poland, and Saudi Arabia for the knockout stage qualification | Group B was another group in the 2022 FIFA World Cup, distinct from Group A, with its own set of teams and matches
Wales is a team that played against the United States in Group B of the 2022 FIFA World Cup, resulting in a draw | Wales ended up last in Group B without advancing to the knockout stage

Poland qualified for the knockout stage on goal difference despite only having 4 points | Poland extended the boycott on 26 February to any qualifying games against Russia | Poland defeated Saudi Arabia and Argentina in Group D, securing their place in the knockout round as runners-up.

Saudi Arabia lost to Poland but defeated Mexico and Argentina, advancing to the knockout stage with a goal difference of -2. | Saudi Arabia ended up last in Group A but secured their place in the knockout stage due to goal difference
Denmark finished last in Group D after losing to France, Tunisia, and Australia. | Denmark is a nation participating in the 2022 FIFA World Cup
Tunisia drew against Denmark but lost to France and Australia, finishing third in Group D. | Tunisia is a nation competing in the 2022 FIFA World Cup
Danish midfielder who made his first major international appearance since suffering a cardiac arrest at UEFA Euro 2020
Australian player who scored the opening goal against France in their match
French professional footballer and forward for Paris Saint-Germain FC, also a member of the French national team
French midfielder who scored one of France's goals against Australia in their match
French striker who scored two goals against Australia to tie with Thierry Henry as France's all-time top goalscorer
Australian forward who scored the only goal in their match against Tunisia, securing their first World Cup win since 2010
Tunisian professional footballer and midfielder for Paris Saint-Germain FC, also a member of the Tunisian national team

Al Janoub Stadium, situated in Al Wakrah, Qatar, was one of the venues for the 2022 FIFA World Cup
Stadium 974, located in Doha, Qatar, hosted a match during the 2022 FIFA World Cup
Group E was one of the groups in the 2022 FIFA World Cup, consisting of teams like Denmark, France, Australia, and Tunisia
Costa Rica is a team that competed in the 2022 FIFA World Cup and lost to Germany, drawing with Spain, and winning against Japan
Spain is a team that also competed in the 2022 FIFA World Cup, losing to Costa Rica, drawing with Germany, and finishing third in their group
Belgium is a team that played in the group stage but was eliminated due to their performance
Canada is a team that participated in the group stage and lost all matches, including an own goal by Nayef Aguerd
Breel Embolo scored a goal for Switzerland against Cameroon, resulting in a 1–0 defeat
Richarlison is a player who scored two goals for Brazil against Serbia

Neymar is a player for Brazil who scored in the quarter-final match against Croatia during extra time. | Neymar is a star player for Brazil and received an ankle injury during the match against Serbia

Cameroon is a team that finished in fourth place and was eliminated from the tournament | Cameroon played in the group stage, scored goals through Jean-Charles Castelletto, Vincent Aboubakar, and Eric Maxim Choupo-Moting, but did not qualify for the knockout stage
Strahinja Pavlović is a player who scored one of Serbia's goals against Cameroon
Sergej Milinković-Savić is a player who scored one of Serbia's goals against Cameroon
Aleksandar Mitrović is a player who scored one of Serbia's goals against Cameroon
Casemiro scored the winning goal for Brazil against Switzerland, helping Brazil qualify for the knockout stage
Vincent Aboubakar is a player who scored Cameroon's only goal in their match against Serbia and was also responsible for Cameroon's disqualification due to his actions after scoring
Switzerland qualified for the knockout stage by defeating Cameroon 1–0, but were defeated by Brazil in their final group game | Switzerland is a team that advanced to the knockout stage of Group G
Serbia did not qualify for the knockout stage after losing their final group game to Brazil 1–0 | Serbia is a team that finished third in Group F and was eliminated from the tournament

Portugal was defeated by Morocco 1-0 in the quarter-finals | Portugal advanced to the knockout stage after finishing first in their group with 6 points, 2 more than Uruguay. | Portugal is a team that advanced to the knockout stage of the tournament

Uruguay is a team that finished third in their group with 1 goal scored, and progressed to the knockout stage after winning against Portugal. | Uruguay is a team that finished third in Group H and was eliminated from the tournament
Group F included teams such as Brazil, Switzerland, Serbia, and Cameroon
Group G included teams such as Portugal, South Korea, Uruguay, and Ghana
Youssef En-Nesyri is a player for Morocco who scored the winning goal against Portugal in the group stage, helping his team advance to the semi-finals.
Michael Oliver is an English referee who officiated a match in the World Cup quarter-finals
Antonio Mateu Lahoz is a Spanish referee who officiated a match between Netherlands and Argentina
Facundo Tello is an Argentine referee who officiated the Morocco vs Portugal match
Wilton Sampaio is a Brazilian referee who officiated the England vs France match
Daniele Orsato is an Italian referee who officiated the Argentina vs Croatia match in the semi-finals
César Arturo Ramos is a Mexican referee who officiated the France vs Morocco match in the semi-finals
The Confederation of African Football (CAF) represents African nations like Morocco
The FIFA World Cup is the international football competition where teams from around the world compete for the title
The Supreme Committee for Delivery and Legacy is responsible for organizing the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar, led by Nasser Al Khater
The 2022 FIFA World Cup was held in Qatar from November to December 2022, with Argentina winning after a penalty shootout against France
Former FIFA president who made controversial statements regarding Qatar's bid and hosted World Cup in 2014.
A venue used for hosting matches during the 2022 FIFA World Cup, located in Doha, Qatar.
A journalist who experienced security issues while reporting live from Katara Cultural Village during the 2022 FIFA World Cup.
A reporter for Irish national broadcaster RTÉ who faced interruptions by Qatari police while filming at the 2022 FIFA World Cup.

Former media officer for the Qatar bid team who claimed that corruption allegations would result in Qatar not hosting the World Cup | Former media officer for Qatar's 2022 World Cup bid, who alleged that three African football officials were offered bribes to support Qatar's bid in a Netflix documentary series "FIFA Uncovered".
The leader of Qatar's 2022 World Cup bid, who has denied allegations of offering bribes to African football officials.
An official from Cameroon's football federation, one of the three African officials allegedly offered bribes by Qatar in 2010 for their support during the World Cup bid process.
An official from Ivory Coast's football federation, another of the three African officials allegedly offered bribes by Qatar in 2010 for their support during the World Cup bid process.
An official from Nigeria's football federation, one of the three African officials allegedly offered bribes by Qatar in 2010 for their support during the World Cup bid process.
A British newspaper that published claims about threats against Qatar and later retracted those claims.
The Secretary-General of the FIFA World Cup Local Organizing Committee for Qatar, who denied allegations of threats from Qatar.
A Swiss media outlet that published an investigative piece about Qatar's espionage operation to secure hosting rights for the 2022 World Cup.
An alleged hacking and smear campaign operation by Qatar to influence FIFA policy and secure the 2022 World Cup hosting rights.
A Zurich-based FIFA insider and consultant for Australia's 2022 World Cup bid who was targeted in a cyberattack.
An entrepreneur associated with the Indian hack-for-hire firm Appin, linked to hacking efforts against critics of Qatar's hosting of the World Cup.

A British newspaper that published an article on labor conditions for workers building infrastructure for the 2022 World Cup. | A British newspaper that published an article surveying workers on their experiences and conditions working on infrastructure for the 2022 World Cup in Qatar.
A sponsorship-based work permit system in Qatar that has been criticized for labor exploitation.
An international organization advocating for workers' rights and criticizing the Kafala system.
President of FIFA, defending Qatar's decision to host the 2022 World Cup
Theo Zwanziger made statements regarding the hosting of the 2022 World Cup in Qatar
Frank Lowy is the chairman of Football Federation Australia and involved in discussions about compensation for the A-League
Richard Scudamore is the chief executive of the Premier League and considering legal action against FIFA
The 2022 FIFA World Cup, scheduled to be held in Qatar
Australian professional football league that would be affected by a change in the World Cup schedule
Confederation of North, Central American and Caribbean Association Football, involved in allegations about the 2022 World Cup bid
Former football official who played a role in securing Qatar's bid for the 2022 World Cup
Jack Warner, former president of CONCACAF, involved in bribery allegations related to the 2022 World Cup bid
Sony, Adidas, Visa, Hyundai, and Coca-Cola are major sponsors of FIFA who called for an investigation into corruption allegations
Swiss officials, with information from the U.S. Department of Justice, arrested several FIFA officials and raided their headquarters
The U.S. Department of Justice provided information leading to the arrests of several FIFA officials
President of the El Salvador soccer association who was sentenced to 16 months in prison for bribery related to the 2022 World Cup bid process
Head of FIFA's Audit and Compliance Committee who stated that evidence of bribery could lead to cancellation of awards
Independent ethics investigator appointed by FIFA to investigate bribery allegations against Russia and Qatar in 2014
German judge who reviewed Michael Garcia's report on the 2018 and 2022 World Cup bid processes
The World Cup is an international football competition organized by FIFA
Russian Anti-Doping Agency found non-compliant for handing over manipulated lab data to investigators
World Anti-Doping Agency, handed Russia a four-year ban from all major sporting events
The Russian national football team was banned from competing in world championships due to non-compliance with WADA rules
Russian Football Union, the national federation of Russia's football team
The Czech Republic announced its unwillingness to play any matches in Russian territory due to the invasion of Ukraine
Sweden made the same decision as Poland, boycotting all matches in Russian territory
Russian Football Union, the governing body for Russian football that faced suspension from FIFA
Rainbow-colored armbands worn by football captains to support anti-discrimination efforts
Issues related to LGBTQ+ rights, including criminalisation of homosexuality and campaigning for LGBTQ+ rights in Qatar
Issues related to women's freedoms and restrictions on their activities in Qatar
A major city in the United States where autopsies are conducted.
An individual who died from an aortic aneurysm rupture, details of which were revealed by an autopsy.
The venue where matches during the 2022 FIFA World Cup took place and where security forces attempted to remove fans wearing certain items or clothing.
A news channel based in London that is critical of Iran's regime and was denied entry to cover the 2022 FIFA World Cup by Qatari authorities.
The name associated with anti-government protests in Iran, whose memory has been used as a symbol for freedom.
A Danish TV presenter who was detained by Qatari police for filming fans with "Woman, Life, Freedom" slogans during the World Cup.>
Supporters of the Iranian government who abused fans displaying protest slogans at the 2026 World Cup in Qatar.>

edges.description: France defeated Argentina 2–0 in the group stage. | Argentina defeated France in the final of the 2022 FIFA World Cup, winning on penalties after a 3-3 draw | Argentina defeated France in the semi-finals of the 2022 World Cup | Argentina defeated France in the final of the 2022 FIFA World Cup | Argentina defeated France 3-2 in the semi-finals but France won the final | France and Argentina competed against each other in the tournament.
Messi scored two goals for Argentina against France in the final | Lionel Messi was an Argentine player who represented Argentina in the 2022 FIFA World Cup and won the Best Player award
Enzo Fernández is a young player from Argentina who received the Best Young Player award at the 2022 FIFA World Cup
Emiliano Martínez, an Argentine goalkeeper, won the Best Goalkeeper award for his performance in the 2022 FIFA World Cup
Argentina is a nation that won the 2022 FIFA World Cup, their third title overall

Messi and Argentina's national team performed well, with Messi winning the Golden Ball. | Lionel Messi is a key player and captain of the Argentine national team. | Messi plays for the Argentine national team
Argentina participated in the 2022 FIFA World Cup
They faced off in the final match of Group A, with Poland qualifying on goal difference
These two teams were in different groups but both advanced to the knockout stage
This stadium hosted Argentina's match against Saudi Arabia
The Netherlands defeated Argentina in the semi-finals to reach the final of the tournament.
Argentina defeated Croatia 3-0 in the quarter-finals
Argentina defeated Morocco in the quarter-finals, while Morocco reached the semi-finals but lost to France
Argentina participated and won the 2022 FIFA World Cup | Argentina won the 2022 FIFA World Cup after defeating France in a penalty shootout
Argentina played against France, a team from Qatar's host nation, in the 2022 World Cup | Qatar hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup where Argentina won, marking their third World Cup win
The Supreme Committee for Delivery and Legacy organized the 2022 FIFA World Cup where Argentina won
Kylian Mbappé is a French player who played for France in the 2022 FIFA World Cup, scoring eight goals and winning the Golden Boot
France reached the final of the 2022 FIFA World Cup and lost to Argentina in penalties after a 3-3 draw
Mbappé plays for the French national team | Kylian Mbappé scored a brace as France defeated Denmark 2–1, securing their place in the knockout round.

France is a participating nation in the 2022 FIFA World Cup and competes against other nations like Denmark, Australia, and Tunisia | France is a member nation participating in FIFA's World Cup tournament
France participated in the 2022 World Cup hosted by Qatar and lost to Argentina in the semi-finals | France participated in the 2022 FIFA World Cup held in Qatar
France defeated Croatia in the 2018 FIFA World Cup Final

France defeated Denmark 2–1 to advance to the knockout round. | France defeated Denmark 2–1 and qualified for the knockout round as one of the top two teams from Group D. | France won against Denmark with a score of 2-1 at Stadium 974 during the 2022 FIFA World Cup, contributing to France's qualification for the knockout stages
Christian Eriksen made his first major international appearance for France after recovering from a cardiac arrest.
Antoine Griezmann equalised for France but his goal was disallowed for offside during a match against Denmark.
Adrien Rabiot scored one of France's goals in their victory over Australia, contributing to the team's qualification.
